title: paeonia rockii subsp. atava
intro: Paeonia rockii subsp. atava is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Paeonia rockii plant, commonly known as the peony. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating menstrual cramps, and promoting relaxation. Herbal preparations of Paeonia rockii subsp. atava typically involve drying the roots and rhizomes, which are then made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal use.
